title: Normative Statement Registry Entry
description: One normative guarantee (a MUST/MUST NOT/SHOULD) or one security claim in the AdCP spec, tagged with where it is enforced. The registry makes 'prose MUST with no enforcement backing' countable. See specs/spec-anti-drift.md.

id.description: Stable ID, NS-<CLASS>-<NNN>, e.g. NS-SIG-001. Never reused.

type.description: normative-statement = a MUST/SHOULD rule; claim = a 'defends against X' assertion in security-facing docs.

class.description: The domain the statement belongs to. Drives the ID prefix and coverage grouping.

level.description: RFC 2119 level as written in the source prose.

statement.description: The normative rule or claim, in one sentence, quoted or closely paraphrased from source.

source.description: file:line where the prose lives. Anchors the statement to its authoritative text.

enforcement_layer.description: The cheapest deterministic layer that should hold this check. UNASSIGNED = triage pending.

status.description: enforced = a linked check exists and passes; gap = known-unenforced (drift surface); operator = punted to operators by design; by-design = unenforceable, documented as such.

enforced_by.description: For normative-statements with status=enforced: repo-relative paths to the schema / test / vector that enforces it. Each path is checked to exist by the coverage linter.

demonstrated_by.description: For claims with status=enforced: repo-relative paths to the test vector or threat-model entry that demonstrates the claim holds.

finding_ref.description: Optional pointer to the audit finding that created this entry, e.g. audit-2026-07/T1-1.

notes.description: Optional context: why it is a gap, what the fix is, dependencies.
